sections.paragraph: , חַצֲצֹן־־תָּמָר (“pruning of the palm”), [Hazazon-tamar, Hazezon-tamar], Gen 14:7 2Ch 20:2, pr.n. of a town situated in the desert of the tribe of Judah, celebrated for its palms; afterwards called עֵין גְּדִי. As to the palms of Engadda, see Plin. H. N. v. 7. Celsii, Hierob. ii. 491.
